title: 5 Beginner Watercolor Mistakes to Avoid
excerpt: Starting with watercolor can be daunting. Here are 5 common mistakes and how to avoid them for better paintings.
body:
Watercolor is a beautiful medium, but it requires patience and a good understanding of water control. 

## 1. Using the wrong paper
Always use watercolor paper (preferably 100% cotton) because regular paper will warp and pill.

## 2. Too much water
It's called watercolor, but using too much water will lead to muddy colors and buckling paper.

## 3. Overworking the paint
Let the layers dry before adding more paint. Overworking leads to muddy colors.

title: Easy DIY Paper Flower Tutorial for Beginners
excerpt: Learn how to make beautiful crepe paper flowers that will last forever with this simple step-by-step guide.
body:
Paper flowers are a fantastic and inexpensive craft. 

## Materials Needed
- Crepe paper
- Floral wire
- Floral tape
- Scissors
- Hot glue

## Step 1: Cut the petals
Start by cutting out petal shapes from your crepe paper. Make sure the grain runs vertically so you can stretch them.
